author: m.kalinin
content: CFIing an EIP on Svalbard is that fast because there is always a daylight?

author: m.kalinin
content: Can we have all the inputs to `engine_newPayload` as public inputs? I believe that if we add anything to that call in the future the guest program would need to be changed as well as if we were having `NewPayloadRequest` root. The only difference is that each time we extend a list of inputs to the `newPayload` we'd have to extend the guest program API call to incorporate a new input

author: taulepton_
content: We can but I think we still need to add beacon block root and blob versioned hashes to the accumulator / partial header as I suspect without them we would have a binding gap for the beacon block roots used to populate the system contract and the blob kzg commitments?

author: taulepton_
content: One could argue that if we have them as part of the accumulator and the CL checks accumulator consistency then what’s the purpose of the fields in engine API?

author: taulepton_
content: I described the partial header commitment as enshrining the engine API into the core protocol. Current EngineAPI consistency checks are local against a single block. Accumulator consistency check is global consistency across all ancestors.

author: taulepton_
content: If we want to introduce a new field to the engineAPI the I believe we would also need to add it to the accumulator for the ancestry consistency checks. Otherwise we have the same problem we’re trying to fix now?

author: taulepton_
content: Thinking out loud here

author: taulepton_
content: In this model you can consider the engineAPI consistency check as being moved from the EL to the CL and also using an accumulator for ancestry consistency

author: m.kalinin
content: If it's a new field to ExecutionPayload or envelope or bid then it shouldn't be added retrospectively?

author: taulepton_
content: I'm not sure if I fully understand the question but I think any new field added to engineAPI would be added to the partial header hash AT the fork boundary the field is added.

author: m.kalinin
content: yes, this is my understanding

author: taulepton_
content:
What are your thoughts regarding:
- adding parent beacon root and blob kzg commitments to the partial header
- redundancy of engine API fields

author: m.kalinin
content:
> adding parent beacon root and blob kzg commitments to the partial header

I think we should add them, like all other fields that must be checked by both layers

author: m.kalinin
content:
> redundancy of engine API fields
What redundancy?

author: m.kalinin
content: Like, if EL can parse blob kzg commitments it doesn't need to get them from the Engine API anymore?

author: taulepton_
content: yes

author: taulepton_
content: I suspect we can pass the execution payload and nothing else in the engineAPI

author: m.kalinin
content: I need to think into it

author: taulepton_
content: sure thing. Let me know your thoughts when you've had a moment to consider

author: m.kalinin
content: right now i would say that if blob kzg commitments and parent beacon root on CL doesn't match the same values on EL side then the new accumulator value would be different between the layers. Thus, seems like we don't need to pass these values

author: m.kalinin
content: Although, `parent_beacon_block_root` is a part of EL block and not included into `ExecutionPayload` so we have to pass it alongside

author: taulepton_
content: I was thinking that we would include parent_beacon_block_root in the partial header hash and then I believe there is an EL system transaction that sets the beacon block root included in every block so we would assert consistency of this parent beacon block root value with the partial header.

author: taulepton_
content: note: I need to look into code to verify this but I believe this is how it would work.

author: m.kalinin
content: If we include `expectedBlobVersionedHashes` and `executionRequests` into a new commitment/accumulator then they may not be passed to the EL, while `parentBeaconBlockRoot` will have to be anyway. There is no system contract where that root is coming from, there is a system call which takes that root and inserts it into a system contract storage to make it usable by dapps.
